title: Facilities, Racks, Cabling, Power, and Environment · Packet Lens: Net Environment Fire
summary: Plan an MDF, IDFs, racks, patching, power, cooling, security, and maintenance access for a new site. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net environment fire increment in a facilities-ready network installation package. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
Calculate UPS runtime, PDU and circuit load, voltage, redundancy, graceful shutdown, and PoE power budget.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Environment Fire
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Environment Fire, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a facilities-ready network installation package while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Any fire extinguisher is safe for energized network equipment. This fails the Net Environment Fire contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Net Environment Fire evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-environment-fire-j4asgg" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Environment Fire into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-environment-fire-j4asgg
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]

stimulus.title: Net Environment Fire failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Any fire extinguisher is safe for energized network equipment.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Net Environment Fire, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Net Environment Fire model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Environment Fire applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
